utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: (1) health_coverage with physical and mental health insurance and costs, (2) property_coverage with home/contents insurance and costs, (3) travel_coverage with travel insurance and costs, (4) additional_services with enhancement services and costs, and (5) total_budget with complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. In the final coverage plan: health_coverage is fully specified with details and monthly cost. property_coverage is detailed but lacks explicit cost information—so not fully specified. travel_coverage includes details and an addon cost, so it is covered. additional_services lists several services but no cost, so not fully specified. total_budget includes monthly and annual cost, so it is fully specified. Thus, 3 items (health_coverage, travel_coverage, total_budget) are fully covered out of 5 total.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three main categories: health_coverage (LifeFirst Standard Plan), property_coverage (matching LifeFirst Standard Coverage based on naming and content), and travel_coverage (matching LifeFirst Standard Plan). From the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan has a rating of 9 for physical health and 4 for mental health; LifeFirst Standard Coverage has a rating of 9 for property protection; LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel insurance has a rating of 9. Additional services listed (telemedicine, wellness programs, gym reimbursement, 24/7 emergency support, group rate opportunities) correspond to health_enhancement_services: Telemedicine Services (rating 10), Wellness Programs (rating 3), Gym Membership Reimbursement (rating 2). Emergency support and group rate opportunities do not have direct rating entries. Therefore, the mapped ratings correspond to the LifeFirst health, property, and travel plans, plus the identified additional services.
